_meta.project: Wonder Boy in Monster World Disassembly
_meta.description: Comprehensive roadmap from the current bootstrap split to a mature source-first Mega Drive disassembly with full 68k coverage, semantic subsystem ownership, classified levels/maps/gfx/text/sound data, and self-contained tooling/docs that describe the intended end state directly.
_meta.current_state: The project has a bit-perfect rebuild, header/source bootstrap, strong early VBlank/menu recovery, and bank-specific owners for all 0x020000+ banks. Bank 0x020000 now exposes a front dialogue/script continuation at 0x020000-0x0201CF, two front bank-relative text/control target families at 0x0201D0-0x020539 and 0x020E4C-0x0211C9, an intervening dialogue/script continuation at 0x02053A-0x020E4B, the already split index/text/name islands through 0x02245F, structured descriptor/index data through 0x022DB7, a partially split bridge at 0x022DB8-0x023AB5, sentinel-delimited word-record families through 0x02482B, and a now-further-decomposed compressed-data tail at 0x02482C-0x03FFFF where the front relative-offset table is source-authored, proven 0xFF7A/0xFF7B block boundaries are explicit through 0x03C6DF, and the former single bank-end remainder at 0x03C6E0-0x03FFFF is now split into five smaller header-like sub-blocks starting at 0x03C6E0, 0x03E846, 0x03EF4E, 0x03F4D1, and 0x03FE24. Bank 0x040000 now also exposes its pre-table front at 0x040000-0x0409F9 as a ROM-order lead-in plus six smaller header-like sub-blocks before the already split flagged ROM-reference tables and same-bank payload targets through 0x07FF66; the strongest repeated-size evidence in that same-bank payload is now cleaner too, with dedicated ROM-order graphics/tile submodules for the earlier isolated 0x045842-0x045D01 block plus the 0x4C0-byte families at 0x04BFAA-0x04E5A9, 0x05C5DA-0x05CA99, 0x05D630-0x05FC2F, and 0x06B192-0x06BB11 after byte review showed every same-bank /usr/bin/bash1-tagged target cleanly fits 38 4bpp planar tiles. Bank 0x080000 still has its cross-bank table-targeted payload front split through 0x0961D7, a structurally split 0x098000-0x09F776 island whose front Z80-like body is now narrowed to 0x098000-0x09907A with two odd-aligned little-endian word lookup tables made explicit at 0x09907B-0x09913C, 0x09913D-0x0991FE, and 0x0991FF, plus a pre-descriptor structural tail through 0x09991F, descriptor/layout bands through 0x099A84, a dedicated command-front owner through 0x09A347, a fully source-visible tail command window through 0x09F776, and the 0x0A0000 tail-bank offset-tree island split through 0x0A4C76.
_meta.last_progress_note: 2026-03-19: renamed bank 0x080000's former mid/a0000 scaffold owners to semantic late-bank modules (`src/bank080000_z80_resources.asm`, `src/bank080000_z80_program.asm`, `src/bank080000_z80_pre_descriptor_data.asm`, `src/bank080000_z80_descriptor_tables.asm`, `src/bank080000_z80_command_front.asm`, `src/bank080000_z80_command_records_{a,b,c}.asm`, and `src/bank080000_offset_tree_payload.asm`) so the late non-fill islands now read by proven Z80-resource and offset-tree payload ownership rather than temporary ROM-position names while preserving the bit-perfect rebuild.

acceptance: Every change preserves a bit-perfect rebuild unless the task explicitly establishes new generated assets that still round-trip to the original bytes.
New labels, constants, and module boundaries follow repo conventions and prefer real game/domain meaning where the ROM proves it.
Recovered code/data ownership is reflected in source files and machine-readable indexes, not only in notes or Ghidra.
Opaque bytes stay opaque only when their format or ownership is still unproven; monolithic bank-sized incbins are not an acceptable end state.

Every still-opaque region is tracked with a current classification such as code, level/map data, compressed graphics, text, script, sound, tables, or fill so the repo steadily converges on human-readable ownership.
editing_rules: Prefer source-authored ASM with labels and comments over coarse incbins once control flow or table structure is proven.
Keep hex literals in dc.b/dc.w/dc.l data so byte identity is easy to audit.
Put RAM, hardware, sound, script, and gameplay constants in the appropriate shared constant files rather than scattering new equates.
Use Node.js for repository tooling and round-trip extract/build helpers.
Prefer semantic subsystem or asset-family filenames over address-stamped bank fragment names once the bytes have a defensible owner or format.

always: Run cmd /c verify.bat after every non-trivial source or data change.
Run node tools/run_checks.js before considering a task complete.
Update rom_map/function indexes/manifests when ROM ownership or labels materially change.
Do not revert unrelated user changes while chasing bit-perfect rebuilds.
before_refactors: Document proven code/data boundaries before moving bytes into new modules.
Confirm branch sizing and absolute-vs-relative control flow remain byte-identical.
Confirm any extracted asset or text bank can rebuild back to the exact original bytes.
Prefer bank/module decomposition and structural naming over cosmetic renaming passes.
Describe final-form module boundaries and naming directly in repo docs and task descriptions, and only promote names and ownership that are supported by Wonder Boy's own ROM evidence.

organization_and_classification_reset.description: Stop the spread of opaque bank-fragment naming and drive the repo toward stable subsystem ownership backed by explicit classification of what each remaining blob actually contains.

tasks.title: Write the target module map for a mature Wonder Boy disassembly
tasks.description: Define the intended steady-state modules for states, gameplay, player, enemy, items, strings, script, mapdata, gfxdata, sound, and other proven subsystems so future work lands in stable destinations instead of new bank-local fragments.

tasks.title: Build a whole-ROM blob classification catalog
tasks.description: Catalog every remaining opaque or semi-opaque ROM region with its current best classification such as 68k code, pointer table, level/map data, compressed graphics, palette/tile metadata, text/dialogue, script/event data, sound/Z80 resources, or fill/padding, plus the evidence and consumer/loader links that support that call.

tasks.title: Rename mature bank-fragment modules to semantic owners
tasks.description: Systematically replace files with names like bank080000_mid_front.asm, bank040000_payload_mid.asm, and similar address- or direction-stamped fragments once their content is understood, keeping only thin ROM-order wrappers while the real logic/data lives in human-meaningful subsystem or asset-family modules.

tasks.title: Classify level/map, graphics, and text ownership before deeper splits
tasks.description: For each bank, explicitly answer which ranges are level or room data, compressed graphics of what, palettes/tilemaps, text/dialogue banks, script/event payloads, and loader metadata so later disassembly work stops treating those families as generic payloads or fronts/tails.

tasks.title: Prevent new placeholder module names from becoming permanent
tasks.description: Add and enforce repo guidance that temporary bank-local slice names are only short-lived scaffolding, must record the intended future owner, and should be retired as soon as a defensible subsystem or asset-family name is available.

disassembly_completion_strategy.description: Reshape the project around final-form module ownership so all later recovery work has stable destinations instead of accumulating inside coarse blobs.

tasks.title: Replace whole-bank ownership with bank-specific include modules
tasks.description: Update src/rom_body.asm so the three 0x020000+ banks are owned by bank-specific source include files rather than direct bank_*.bin incbins, even if each new module still contains smaller temporary slices at first.

tasks.title: Define target module map for a mature Wonder Boy split
tasks.description: Design the intended steady-state source layout by mapping bytes into stable owners such as gameplay, states, player, enemy, items, strings, script, mapdata, gfxdata, sound, and other proven subsystems instead of ad hoc bank fragments.

tasks.title: Break init and core coarse blobs into subsystem module frontiers
tasks.description: Replace the single init/core ownership model with explicit subsystem frontiers that separate startup, state dispatch, menus, gameplay, map loading, rendering helpers, script systems, and bank-local continuations without changing ROM order.

tasks.title: Upgrade ROM ownership metadata to module-level granularity
tasks.description: Expand tools/index/rom_map.json so it stops describing late ROM as three opaque banks and instead tracks every source-authored slice, data table, extracted asset block, and remaining opaque region at a reviewable granularity.

tasks.title: Write a parity target checklist for mature disassembly state
tasks.description: Document the concrete end-state criteria for this project: all 68k code source-authored, all major data tables named, all text/script systems decoded, all remaining incbins justified as assets, and all indexes/tooling updated to reflect that maturity.

core_frontier_recovery.description: Finish the remaining low-ROM executable frontier and expand the early-game code split until the entire 0x000200-0x01FFFF region is source-authored and sensibly modularized.

tasks.title: Source-author the 0x0088C2-0x008C33 gauge and spell-bar block
tasks.description: Finish the current rendering-heavy frontier in src/core.asm, proving code/data boundaries across the gauge writer, inline scale bundles, tile tables, and width synthesis so the remaining magic/status UI body stops depending on a coarse incbin.

tasks.title: Recover startup and bootstrap flow from 0x000200-0x004B57
tasks.description: Disassemble and label the entire init blob so reset/startup, hardware init, RAM clears, checksum or header handling, decompressor/bootstrap helpers, and early scene setup are all source-authored instead of living in init_000200_004b57.bin.

tasks.title: Recover the 0x005261-0x007923 core continuation
tasks.description: Disassemble the large post-bootstrap core gap so the control flow from controller/input helpers into downstream menu, state, and gameplay systems is visible and no longer hidden behind a single mid-core incbin.

tasks.title: Recover the full 0x008C80-0x01FFFF low-ROM body
tasks.description: Disassemble the rest of the low ROM into source-authored code and named tables, splitting it into steady-state modules such as states, gameplay, player, enemy, map/script, and misc data as real subsystem ownership becomes clear.
tasks.file: src/core.asm + src/states.asm + src/gameplay.asm + src/player.asm + src/enemy.asm + src/miscdata.asm

tasks.title: Recover high-level state dispatchers and scene transitions
tasks.description: Identify and name the main game state machine, mode entry points, submenu dispatchers, scripted transition paths, and return conventions so module boundaries follow real behavior rather than raw address ranges.

tasks.title: Audit all computed control flow in recovered 68k code
tasks.description: Systematically identify jump tables, function-pointer dispatch, fallthrough shared tails, and dead-code candidates across the recovered low-ROM code so no hidden control-flow edges remain masked by conservative splitting.

tasks.title: Eliminate active-code incbins below 0x020000
tasks.description: Drive the entire low-ROM code region to a mature state where any remaining incbins below 0x020000 are proven asset/data islands rather than executable 68k code, with comments and manifests explaining every exception.

bank_020000_recovery.description: Turn the first fully opaque mid-ROM bank into a named mix of source-authored code, decoded tables, and only narrowly-scoped asset incbins.

tasks.title: Create a bank020000 include tree and ownership skeleton
tasks.description: Add src/bank020000.asm and any initial child modules so the 0x020000-0x03FFFF region has a stable source owner before deep recovery starts, with room for code, strings, scripts, and asset sections.

tasks.title: Map code, table, text, and asset boundaries across bank 0x020000
tasks.description: Survey the entire bank to distinguish executable 68k code, pointer tables, text/dialogue banks, compressed blocks, graphics/tile data, and padding/fill so the bank stops being treated as a single unknown blob.

tasks.title: Recover all executable 68k routines in bank 0x020000
tasks.description: Disassemble and label every proven 68k routine in the first bank, including dispatchers, helper routines, and bank-local submodules, until no executable code in 0x020000-0x03FFFF remains hidden in raw incbins.

tasks.title: Recover pointer tables and structured data in bank 0x020000
tasks.description: Convert jump tables, pointer tables, offset tables, descriptor tables, and other structured data in the bank into named assembly tables with documented entry formats and stable labels.

tasks.title: Extract and name text-bearing regions in bank 0x020000
tasks.description: Find every text-bearing region in the bank, identify which ones are menus, shops, dialogue, signposts, item names, or credits-related strings, and move them into structured text ownership instead of leaving them inside bank_020000_03ffff.bin.

tasks.title: Recover script or event data formats in bank 0x020000
tasks.description: Identify any script bytecode, cutscene/event data, branching tables, or trigger lists living in this bank and move them into documented script/data modules with entry-point labels and format notes.

tasks.title: Reduce bank 0x020000 raw coverage to proven asset islands only
tasks.description: End the bank recovery with only tightly-scoped, format-justified asset or compressed-data incbins remaining, each surrounded by named loaders, pointer owners, and manifest entries.

bank_040000_recovery.description: Decode the large late-ROM bank into modules that make its code/data mix understandable and editable.

tasks.title: Create a bank040000 include tree and ownership skeleton
tasks.description: Add src/bank040000.asm and initial child modules so the 0x040000-0x07FFFF region can be split incrementally without keeping a whole-bank incbin in the top-level ROM body.

tasks.title: Map the full structure of bank 0x040000
tasks.description: Identify where code, pointer tables, compressed assets, dialogue/text blocks, map data, and fill regions actually live in 0x040000-0x07FFFF so recovery work can proceed by proven structure instead of guesswork.

tasks.title: Recover all executable 68k routines in bank 0x040000
tasks.description: Disassemble every real 68k routine in the bank, including gameplay continuations, subsystem helpers, and bank-local dispatchers, so no executable logic in 0x040000-0x07FFFF remains trapped in bank_040000_07ffff.bin.

tasks.title: Recover all pointer and descriptor tables in bank 0x040000
tasks.description: Convert late-ROM pointer arrays, descriptor tables, animation tables, map/object references, and table-driven state data into documented assembly structures with validated entry counts and owners.

tasks.title: Recover dialogue, script, and UI text hosted in bank 0x040000
tasks.description: Locate and split any dialogue/script/text resources in this bank, especially story scenes, location text, shop/inn text, and credits-linked strings, with pointer-table ownership and round-trip validation.

tasks.title: Recover late-ROM asset loaders and their data owners in bank 0x040000
tasks.description: Tie compressed blocks, map/tile data, sound-related resources, and other large blobs in this bank to their loading code and pointer owners so extracted assets are no longer orphaned raw slices.

tasks.title: Reduce bank 0x040000 raw coverage to justified assets only
tasks.description: Drive the bank to the same mature end-state as the lower banks: executable code fully source-authored, tables named, text split out, and any remaining incbins narrowed to well-documented asset payloads.

bank_080000_recovery.description: Decode the tail banks with their heavy fill regions so the project still reaches a mature, justified split instead of leaving the last quarter of the ROM opaque.

tasks.title: Create a bank080000 include tree and ownership skeleton
tasks.description: Add src/bank080000.asm and child modules so the tail 0x080000-0x0BFFFF region stops being owned directly by a whole-bank incbin and can be decomposed into smaller proven slices.

tasks.title: Map fill, padding, data, and code boundaries in bank 0x080000
tasks.description: Document which long 0xFF runs are true padding, sparse tables, empty bank space, or encoded assets, and identify any executable 68k code or pointer tables hidden among those tail-bank regions.

tasks.title: Recover any executable 68k logic present in bank 0x080000
tasks.description: Disassemble and label all proven code in the tail banks, including helper routines, data-driven interpreters, or loader code, so no real 68k execution remains hidden in the last quarter of the ROM.

tasks.title: Recover structured tables and asset ownership in bank 0x080000
tasks.description: Name and split all pointer tables, terminal data banks, credits/endgame resources, or tail-end asset blocks in the 0x080000-0x0BFFFF region, with manifests describing any remaining opaque formats.

tasks.title: Reduce bank 0x080000 to explained data ownership
tasks.description: End tail-bank recovery with every byte either source-authored, described as padding/fill, or attached to a named asset family and loader path, so the last quarter of the ROM is no longer a black box.

text_and_script_recovery.description: Decode every textual and scripted system so dialogue, UI, progression logic, and localization details are no longer trapped inside unknown byte arrays.

tasks.title: Reverse the full text encoding and control-code scheme
tasks.description: Identify character encoding, terminators, inline control codes, line-break behavior, text speed/control bytes, and any context-sensitive glyph handling used across dialogue, menus, signs, and credits.

tasks.title: Recover font and glyph mapping ownership
tasks.description: Locate the font graphics, tile-to-character mapping, width or layout metadata, and any banked font variants so decoded text is grounded in repo-local data instead of external dump assumptions.

tasks.title: Build a repo-local full text dump and round-trip builder
tasks.description: Create tools/text_dump.js and tools/text_build.js so every known text bank can be decoded into readable files, edited, and rebuilt back to the exact original bytes with pointer-table stability checks.

tasks.title: Classify and split all menu, item, and magic strings
tasks.description: Move UI strings such as inventory headings, spell names, item names, equipment names, prompts, and status labels into structured string modules with stable labels and pointer ownership.

tasks.title: Classify and split all dialogue, shop, inn, and sign text
tasks.description: Recover story dialogue and world text into named banks or files grouped by subsystem, town, area, or event, using proven pointer tables and known localized names only where the ROM supports them.

tasks.title: Reverse the event and script opcode formats
tasks.description: Identify script commands, argument formats, pointer semantics, branching rules, and event trigger integration so story and progression logic become readable instead of remaining opaque byte streams.

tasks.title: Split scripts into source-owned modules with entry labels
tasks.description: Once script formats are understood, move script banks and event entry tables into named modules with stable script entry labels, caller references, and documentation of bank/area ownership.

tasks.title: Add text and script validation tooling
tasks.description: Create automated checks for text pointer validity, encoded length limits, control-code sanity, and script entry coverage so recovered text/script systems remain safe to edit.

data_table_recovery.description: Promote every major structured data family into named tables with documented formats so the ROM is semantically organized rather than just mechanically rebuildable.

tasks.title: Create a full pointer-table and descriptor-table inventory
tasks.description: Catalog every pointer table, offset table, descriptor table, and repeated-record structure in the ROM, noting ROM range, owner, entry size, likely consumer, and current confidence level.

tasks.title: Recover inventory, equipment, item, and magic data tables
tasks.description: Identify the actual data tables that drive item names, equipment attributes, consumable behavior, magic definitions, and category availability so menu/UI recovery is backed by real structured ownership.

tasks.title: Recover enemy, boss, and combat parameter tables
tasks.description: Locate enemy stat tables, boss parameter tables, AI descriptor tables, projectile or hitbox data, and combat lookup structures so action-RPG behavior is driven by named data instead of anonymous bytes.

tasks.title: Recover shop, inn, and economy tables
tasks.description: Identify shop inventories, prices, inn pricing/behavior, and economy-related tables so all merchant-facing systems are represented as named data with town or owner context where proven.

tasks.title: Recover room, area, warp, and progression tables
tasks.description: Find the tables that define areas, room transitions, warps, exits, and world progression links so map and script recovery can rely on named structural data instead of scattered raw pointers.

tasks.title: Recover object spawn, trigger, and event placement tables
tasks.description: Identify the structured data that places NPCs, enemies, triggers, chests, doors, and scripted interactions in each area, including entry formats and pointer ownership.

tasks.title: Recover animation, sprite-composition, and render descriptor tables
tasks.description: Document the table formats that drive animation sequences, sprite piece layouts, tile compositions, and render-side descriptor data for player, enemies, UI, and effects.

tasks.title: Recover save, options, and persistent-state data structures
tasks.description: Determine whether the game truly uses backup RAM or save-related structures beyond the header artifacts, and if so, map the save layout, checks, and persistent progression tables in source and documentation.

format_and_asset_recovery.description: Decode the non-code formats so remaining incbins become deliberate asset containers with extract/build pipelines rather than unexplained leftovers.

tasks.title: Reverse all compression and decompression formats used by the ROM
tasks.description: Identify every decompressor, the data families it serves, and the exact format rules, then build extraction and rebuild tooling that reproduces each compressed block byte-for-byte.

tasks.title: Recover graphics, palette, and tilemap formats
tasks.description: Document sprite/tile encoding, palette storage, map tilemap layouts, UI tile banks, and any chunk/pointer schemes so art and layout data can be extracted and understood as real formats.

tasks.title: Recover map encodings and area asset pipelines
tasks.description: Decode overworld, town, dungeon, and room data encodings, including any compression, metatile layers, collision layers, and entity references, with round-trip extraction/build tooling.

tasks.title: Recover sound driver structure and 68000/Z80 interface
tasks.description: Document the 68000-to-sound-driver handshake, sound command format, Z80 boundaries, bank ownership, and sound-driver RAM usage so sound code can be split into source and named data rather than remaining a future black box.

tasks.title: Separate sound assets from sound code and command tables
tasks.description: Identify sequence data, DAC/sample payloads, FM/PSG resources, and any sound lookup tables so sound-related incbins are partitioned into named code and asset ownership.

tasks.title: Create per-family asset manifests for extracted blob ranges
tasks.description: For text, maps, art, sound, and other asset families, maintain manifests recording ROM ranges, format, parent pointers/loaders, rebuild tools, and unresolved questions so remaining opaque data is always contextualized.

tasks.title: Externalize mature asset families into structured data directories
tasks.description: Once formats and round-trip tooling exist, move large art, map, sound, and script resources out of generic data/rom blobs into structured data trees without losing ROM-order traceability or bit-perfect rebuild behavior.

symbolization_and_docs.description: Raise naming, comments, RAM/constant coverage, and structural documentation until the codebase reads like a maintained disassembly instead of a partially decoded binary mirror.

tasks.title: Expand the RAM map from local anchors to subsystem-level structures
tasks.description: Continue naming work RAM so player, object, menu, map, script, render, task, and sound work areas are represented as real structs or grouped regions rather than isolated address notes.

tasks.title: Define comprehensive gameplay and subsystem constants
tasks.description: Create or expand shared constant files for gameplay states, object types, menu ids, script opcodes, sound commands, tile attributes, and table sizes so recovered code stops depending on unexplained immediates.

tasks.title: Replace raw struct offsets with named field constants
tasks.description: Systematically convert proven $NN(Ax) patterns into named field offsets for player, object, menu, map, script, and save-related structures across all recovered modules.

tasks.title: Eliminate placeholder or address-suffixed labels in mature modules
tasks.description: As each module reaches stable understanding, replace conservative address-suffixed or generic behavior labels with stronger subsystem or game-facing names while preserving cross-reference traceability in comments or indexes.

tasks.title: Add function headers and module-level documentation throughout src
tasks.description: Give each mature module clear file headers, exported routine summaries, register usage notes, and data format comments so reverse engineering knowledge lives in source instead of only in worklogs.

tasks.title: Document every misplaced data island caused by ROM-order constraints
tasks.description: Whenever a function or table must stay in an apparently odd file because of bit-perfect ROM ordering, add prominent comments explaining the physical-ROM reason and the logical owner.

tasks.title: Create docs for module boundaries, table style, and naming rules
tasks.description: Write repo-specific guidance for how modules are split, how data tables should be represented, and how domain names should be introduced so future recovery stays consistent and reviewable.

tasks.title: Keep Ghidra and source labels synchronized as source matures
tasks.description: Expand the source-to-Ghidra sync process so labels, comments, bookmarks, and module ownership remain aligned between the repo and the analysis project as large bank recoveries land.

tooling_and_indexes.description: Build the indexes, viewers, validators, and extraction tools needed to support mature reverse engineering and future editing without relying on manual address chasing.

tasks.title: Expand the function index to full-ROM coverage
tasks.description: Upgrade tools/index/function_index.json so it tracks every recovered routine, entry point, dispatcher, and table-dispatched target across all banks rather than only the current early-ROM frontier.

tasks.title: Generate callsite, include-graph, and RAM-symbol indexes
tasks.description: Add repo-native generators for callsites, include graph, and RAM symbol coverage so control flow, module dependencies, and memory ownership can be audited the same way mature disassemblies do.

tasks.title: Generate indexes for strings, scripts, and data tables
tasks.description: Create machine-readable indexes for decoded strings, script entries/opcodes, and structured tables so tooling and reviewers can navigate text/data ownership without scanning raw assembly.

tasks.title: Build extraction and injection CLIs for mature data families
tasks.description: Create command-line tooling that can dump and rebuild recovered text, scripts, maps, and gameplay tables so future editing work uses verified pipelines instead of manual source edits alone.
tasks.file: tools/text_dump.js + tools/text_build.js + tools/map_extract.js + tools/map_build.js + tools/extract_game_data.js + tools/inject_game_data.js

tasks.title: Extend viewers to cover decoded maps, palettes, text, and tables
tasks.description: Turn the existing bootstrap viewers into a broader inspection suite that can render recovered maps, palettes, strings, and table-driven gameplay data in human-readable form.

tasks.title: Add lints for raw struct offsets, table formatting, and asset manifest drift
tasks.description: Extend run_checks so it warns or fails on newly introduced raw struct offsets, malformed named tables, missing manifest metadata, and other regressions that would erode a mature source layout.

tasks.title: Verify metadata freshness against current source ownership
tasks.description: Expand verify_metadata.js so it checks that symbol maps, ROM ownership manifests, table indexes, and other generated metadata are up to date after major source splits.

tasks.title: Create a maturity dashboard for coverage of code, labels, and tables
tasks.description: Produce a report summarizing source-authored ROM coverage, remaining incbin ranges, label quality, table coverage, and decoded asset families so progress toward a fully mapped disassembly is measurable.

regression_and_parity.description: Harden the repo so the long push from coarse split to mature disassembly stays reproducible, reviewable, and aligned with the desired end state.

tasks.title: Create a pre-commit verification hook for Wonderboy
tasks.description: Add Windows and shell pre-commit helpers that run the standard validation and bit-perfect checks before commits so regressions are caught before they enter history.

tasks.title: Document the workflow for diagnosing verify failures after deep bank splits
tasks.description: Write a repo-specific guide for reading listing files, using rom_diff output, checking branch sizing, and debugging off-by-one boundary mistakes that appear during large source-authored bank conversions.

tasks.title: Refresh onboarding and contributor docs for the mature workflow
tasks.description: Replace the bootstrap-era guidance with documentation that explains module ownership, bank recovery expectations, tooling/index refresh steps, and how to add new source-authored slices safely.

tasks.title: Refresh milestone gates for full-disassembly completion
tasks.description: Rewrite docs/milestones.md so it tracks the transition from coarse bootstrap to complete 68k coverage, full data-table recovery, decoded asset formats, and mature tooling rather than stopping at early scaffold milestones.

tasks.title: Perform periodic parity audits against the target mature state
tasks.description: Establish a recurring review that measures Wonderboy against the intended mature-disassembly checklist: module coverage, label quality, table ownership, text/script extraction, and tooling completeness.

tasks.title: Close the project only when every remaining incbin is justified
tasks.description: Make the final acceptance criterion explicit: the project is not considered mature until every surviving incbin is a documented asset or padding region with known owner, and all executable 68k plus structured tables are source-authored and indexed.
